raw_consumer_statement:
I canceled my American Express card shortly after a {$130.00} payment attempt failed due to insufficient funds. Before the failed payment was ever resolved, Amex refunded the {$320.00} annual fee on my account and applied a {$75.00} credit. 

Despite this, I was later told I owe {$130.00}. I requested an itemized breakdown multiple times, and only after escalation to a supervisor was I told the balance includes : {$40.00} for a gym charge ( which I acknowledge ), {$75.00} as a reversal of the credit they gave me, And {$27.00} as a return payment fee for the failed payment attempt. 

This {$27.00} fee feels unfair and predatory. The payment was made in good faith before I canceled the account, but the refund issued afterward made that payment unnecessary. Penalizing me for a failed payment toward a balance that no longer existed seems unethical. 

I am requesting a formal review and that Amex waive or remove this {$27.00} return fee.
